 {"id":"beads_rust-1md","title":"Phase 4: Sync & Config - JSONL Import/Export, Configuration","description":"# Phase 4: Sync & Config

## Goals
Implement JSONL import/export, auto-import/flush, and configuration systems with metadata.json handling.

## Deliverables
- JSONL export + import (classic rules)
- Auto-flush + dirty tracking + export hashes
- Auto-import staleness detection (Lstat + hash)
- `sync --flush-only` / `--import-only`
- Config system (YAML + DB) + `config` command
- JSONL discovery + metadata.json
- `--no-db` mode

## Acceptance Criteria
- JSONL round-trip matches bd.
- Staleness checks + conflict detection behave correctly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T06:10:53.264405317Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T08:41:37.145117022Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T08:41:37.145117022Z","close_reason":"All Phase 4 deliverables complete: JSONL export/import, sync command (--flush-only/--import-only), config system with YAML+DB layering, --no-db JSONL-only mode, auto-flush + dirty tracking.  {"id":"beads_rust-1mu2","title":"Create README.md with quick-start guide","description":"# README.md Creation

## Purpose
Create a comprehensive README.md that serves as the project landing page, enabling users to understand and start using br within 30 seconds.

## Structure

### Header Section
```markdown
# br - Beads Rust 🦀

A fast, non-invasive issue tracker for git repositories. Rust port of [beads](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads).

[\![CI](https://github.com/.../actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](...)
[\![Crates.io](https://img.shields.io/crates/v/beads-rust.svg)](...)
[\![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](...)
```

### Quick Install
```markdown
## Quick Install

\`\`\`bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../install.sh | bash
\`\`\`

Or with Homebrew:
\`\`\`bash
brew install dicklesworthstone/tap/br
\`\`\`
```

### TL;DR
```markdown
## TL;DR

br is a local-first issue tracker that stores issues in SQLite with JSONL export for git-based collaboration. It's designed to be non-invasive: no daemons, no git hooks, no auto-commits.

\`\`\`bash
br init                              # Initialize in current repo
br create "Fix login bug" -p 1       # Create high-priority bug
br list                              # Show all issues
br ready                             # Show actionable work
br close bd-abc123                   # Close an issue
br sync --flush-only                 # Export to JSONL for git
\`\`\`
```

### Features Table
```markdown
## Features

| Feature | Status | Description |
|---------|--------|-------------|
| Issue CRUD | ✅ | Create, read, update, delete issues |
| Dependencies | ✅ | Block/unblock relationships |
| Labels | ✅ | Categorize with custom labels |
| Search | ✅ | Full-text search across issues |
| JSONL Sync | ✅ | Git-friendly export/import |
| AI Agent Mode | 🚧 | Structured output for AI tools |
| TUI Mode | 📋 | Interactive terminal UI |
```

### AI Agent Integration
```markdown
## AI Agent Integration

br is designed to work seamlessly with AI coding agents like Claude Code:

\`\`\`bash
# Get machine-readable help
br --robot-help

# Get prioritized work for agent
br --robot-triage

# Structured JSON output
br list --json
\`\`\`

See [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) for complete agent integration guide.
```

### Quick Example
```markdown
## Quick Example

\`\`\`bash
# Initialize br in your project
cd my-project
br init

# Create your first issue
br create --title "Implement user authentication" --type feature --priority 1

# Add a dependency
br create --title "Set up database schema" --type task
br dep add bd-xyz bd-abc  # xyz depends on abc

# See what's ready to work on
br ready

# Claim work
br update bd-abc --status in_progress

# Complete and sync
br close bd-abc --reason "Schema implemented"
br sync --flush-only
git add .beads/ && git commit -m "Update issues"
\`\`\`
```

### Architecture
```markdown
## Architecture

\`\`\`
┌─────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐
│   CLI (br)  │────▶│ SQLite Store │────▶│ JSONL Sync  │
└─────────────┘     └──────────────┘     └─────────────┘
                           │                    │
                           ▼                    ▼
                    .beads/beads.db      .beads/issues.jsonl
\`\`\`

- **SQLite**: Primary storage, WAL mode, concurrent access
- **JSONL**: Git-friendly export for collaboration
- **No daemon**: Simple CLI, no background processes
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] README.md exists at project root
- [ ] Installation instructions are correct
- [ ] Quick example works as written
- [ ] All links are valid
- [ ] Badges display correctly
- [ ] Mobile-friendly (readable on GitHub mobile)

## Dependencies
- Installation script (for accurate install commands)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenPuma","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T18:51:07.257879900Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T22:54:31.424817281Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T22:54:31.424817281Z","close_reason":"Created comprehensive README.md with quick-start guide, features table, AI agent integration, architecture diagram, safety model reference, and full command reference. Fixed LICENSE link. All other links verified.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
-{"id":"beads_rust-1pt5","title":"Show design, notes, acceptance_criteria, external_ref in br show text output (GH#61)","description":"format_issue_details in show.rs only displays description. Add labeled sections for design, notes, acceptance_criteria, and external_ref when populated, matching the original bd show output.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:18:36.956111745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:18:36.956111745Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"beads_rust-1pt5","title":"Show design, notes, acceptance_criteria, external_ref in br show text output (GH#61)","description":"## Problem

`format_issue_details` in `src/cli/commands/show.rs:95-173` only displays: status icon, ID, title, priority, status, owner, type, created/updated dates, assignee, labels, description, dependencies, dependents, and comments. It omits 5 user-facing fields that are commonly populated: `design`, `acceptance_criteria`, `notes`, `external_ref`, and metadata like `due_at`, `defer_until`, `estimated_minutes`, `closed_at`, `close_reason`.

The JSON output (`--json`) shows all fields via serde flatten of the Issue struct, but the human-readable text output is incomplete, making `br show` less useful than the old `bd show`.

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Add primary content fields to format_issue_details (show.rs:137-140)

After the description section (line 140), add sections for design, acceptance_criteria, and notes. Follow the same pattern as description:

```rust
if let Some(design) = &issue.design {
    if !design.is_empty() {
        lines.push(String::new());
        lines.push(format!("{}Design:{}", style.dim, style.reset));
        for line in design.lines() {
            lines.push(format!("  {line}"));
        }
    }
}
// Same pattern for acceptance_criteria ("Acceptance Criteria:") and notes ("Notes:")
```

### 2. Add metadata fields to header section (show.rs:114-135)

After the labels line, add conditional display for:
- `external_ref` (e.g., "Ref: GH#42")
- `due_at` (e.g., "Due: 2026-03-01")
- `defer_until` (e.g., "Deferred until: 2026-02-15")
- `estimated_minutes` (e.g., "Estimate: 2h 30m")

### 3. Add closure info for closed issues (show.rs:122-127)

After the created/updated line, if `closed_at` is Some:
- Show "Closed: 2026-02-10 (reason: completed)"
- Include `close_reason` if present

### 4. Preserve JSON output unchanged

The JSON output path (show.rs:66-68) already serializes all fields. No changes needed there.

## Files to Modify

- `src/cli/commands/show.rs` lines 95-173 (format_issue_details function)
- No model changes needed (fields already exist on Issue struct)","acceptance_criteria":"1. `br show` displays design section when populated (labeled "Design:")
2. `br show` displays acceptance_criteria section when populated (labeled "Acceptance Criteria:")
3. `br show` displays notes section when populated (labeled "Notes:")
4. `br show` displays external_ref when populated (inline, e.g., "Ref: GH#42")
5. `br show` displays due_at when set
6. `br show` displays defer_until when set
7. `br show` displays estimated_minutes when set (formatted as hours+minutes)
8. `br show` displays closed_at and close_reason for closed issues
9. Empty/None fields are not displayed (no blank sections)
10. JSON output unchanged
11. All existing tests pass","notes":"## Unit Tests

1. **test_show_design_field**: Create issue with --design "Design notes". Run `br show`. Assert output contains "Design:" header and content.
2. **test_show_acceptance_criteria**: Create issue with --acceptance "AC1\nAC2". Run `br show`. Assert "Acceptance Criteria:" section present.
3. **test_show_notes_field**: Create issue with --notes "Implementation notes". Assert "Notes:" section present.
4. **test_show_external_ref**: Create issue, update with external_ref. Assert "Ref:" appears in header.
5. **test_show_empty_fields_hidden**: Create minimal issue (no design/notes/AC). Assert none of those sections appear.
6. **test_show_closed_issue_metadata**: Create issue, close it. Assert "Closed:" line appears with date.
7. **test_show_due_date**: Create issue with --due "2026-03-01". Assert "Due:" appears.
8. **test_show_deferred**: Defer issue. Assert "Deferred until:" appears.
9. **test_show_json_unchanged**: Create issue with all fields. Run `br show --json`. Assert all fields present in JSON (regression test).

## E2E Test Script (scripts/test_show_fields.sh)

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
PASS=0; FAIL=0
TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d)
cd "$TMPDIR" && br init --force >/dev/null 2>&1

# Create issue with all fields
ID=$(br q "Full field test")
br update "$ID" --design "System design here" --acceptance "AC1: Must work" --notes "Extra notes"

# Test: design shown
OUTPUT=$(br show "$ID" 2>&1)
echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "Design:" && ((PASS++)) || { echo "FAIL: Design not shown"; ((FAIL++)); }
echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "Acceptance Criteria:" && ((PASS++)) || { echo "FAIL: AC not shown"; ((FAIL++)); }
echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "Notes:" && ((PASS++)) || { echo "FAIL: Notes not shown"; ((FAIL++)); }

# Test: minimal issue hides empty fields
ID2=$(br q "Minimal issue")
OUTPUT2=$(br show "$ID2" 2>&1)
echo "$OUTPUT2" | grep -qv "Design:" && ((PASS++)) || { echo "FAIL: Design shown when empty"; ((FAIL++)); }

echo "Results: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed"
[[ $FAIL -eq 0 ]] && echo "ALL PASS" || exit 1
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:18:36.956111745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T07:42:21.393572648Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-1qeq","title":"Create multi-platform installer script","description":"# Multi-Platform Installer Script

## Purpose
Enable one-liner installation of br on Linux, macOS, and Windows with automatic platform detection, checksum verification, and idempotent behavior.

## Technical Requirements

### Script Features
- Platform detection (Linux/macOS/Windows)
- Architecture detection (x86_64/arm64)
- Download from GitHub Releases
- SHA256 checksum verification
- Fallback to source build if binary unavailable
- Idempotent (safe to re-run)
- Lock mechanism for concurrent protection
- Resume capability for interrupted downloads
- PATH modification with shell detection
- Proxy support (HTTPS_PROXY)

### Installation Methods
```bash
# Primary: curl
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust/main/install.sh | bash

# Alternative: wget
wget -qO- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust/main/install.sh | bash

# With options
curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash -s -- --prefix=~/.local --no-modify-path
```

### Script Structure
```bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Configuration
REPO="Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust"
BINARY_NAME="br"
INSTALL_DIR="${HOME}/.local/bin"

# Functions
detect_platform()     # linux_amd64, darwin_arm64, etc.
detect_shell()        # bash, zsh, fish
download_release()    # curl with retry
verify_checksum()     # sha256sum verification
install_binary()      # atomic install
modify_path()         # idempotent PATH update
build_from_source()   # fallback with Rust install

# Main
main() {
    parse_args "$@"
    acquire_lock
    trap cleanup EXIT
    
    PLATFORM="$(detect_platform)"
    
    if \! download_release "$PLATFORM"; then
        warn "Binary not available, building from source..."
        build_from_source
    fi
    
    verify_checksum
    install_binary
    [[ "$MODIFY_PATH" == "true" ]] \u0026\u0026 modify_path
    
    echo "✓ br installed successfully\!"
    echo "  Run 'br --help' to get started."
}
```

### Checksum Verification
```bash
verify_checksum() {
    local expected="$(curl -fsSL "${RELEASE_URL}.sha256")"
    local actual="$(sha256sum "$DOWNLOAD_PATH" | cut -d' ' -f1)"
    
    if [[ "$expected" \!= "$actual" ]]; then
        error "Checksum mismatch\! Expected: $expected, Got: $actual"
        exit 1
    fi
}
```

### Idempotency Patterns
- mkdir -p (creates if not exists)
- Check before PATH modify (grep for existing entry)
- Lock file with stale detection
- Atomic moves (mv, not cp)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Works on Ubuntu 22.04+, macOS 13+, Windows 11 (WSL)
- [ ] Detects x86_64 and arm64 architectures
- [ ] Verifies checksums before install
- [ ] Falls back to source build gracefully
- [ ] Idempotent (multiple runs don't break)
- [ ] Modifies PATH correctly for bash/zsh/fish
- [ ] Works behind HTTPS_PROXY
- [ ] Clear error messages on failure

## Files to Create
- `install.sh` - Main installer script
- `scripts/build-release.sh` - Build script for releases
- `.github/workflows/release.yml` - Release automation

## References
- ACFS install.sh patterns
- self_update crate documentation
- Homebrew installer conventions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"StormyBarn","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:10.392094598Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T03:52:49.473288374Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T03:52:49.473191582Z","external_ref":"second-min","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}

-{"id":"beads_rust-33eh","title":"Return non-zero exit code from br close for already-closed issues (GH#62)","description":"br close returns exit code 0 when all provided issues are already closed. Should return non-zero when no issues were successfully closed, matching behavior for non-existent IDs (exit code 3).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:18:32.708788786Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:18:32.708788786Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"beads_rust-33eh","title":"Return non-zero exit code from br close for already-closed issues (GH#62)","description":"## Problem

`br close` returns exit code 0 when all provided issues are already closed. It should return non-zero when no issues were successfully transitioned, matching the expected behavior for scripting and CI pipelines.

## Root Cause

In `src/cli/commands/close.rs:188-204`, both non-existent IDs and already-closed issues are added to `skipped_issues` vec without raising an error. The function completes successfully with exit 0 regardless of whether any issues were actually closed. The error module (`src/error/mod.rs:245-248`) maps all errors to exit code 1 via a single `exit_code()` method.

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Add exit code logic to close command (close.rs)

After processing all IDs, check if any were successfully closed. If `closed_count == 0 && skipped_issues.len() > 0`:
- Print the skip reasons as currently done
- Return a new error variant or set process exit code to 3 (matching the convention for "nothing to do")

### 2. Add new error variant or exit code (error/mod.rs)

Option A (preferred): Add `NothingToDo` variant to the error enum with exit code 3:
```rust
NothingToDo { message: String } => exit_code 3
```

Option B: Add specific exit code handling in close command using `std::process::exit(3)` after printing output. Less clean but simpler.

### 3. Ensure backward compat for mixed success

When some IDs succeed and some are skipped, exit 0 (partial success is still success). Only exit 3 when ALL provided IDs were skipped.

### 4. Update JSON output

The JSON output already includes `skipped` array. Add a `closed_count` field so scripts can programmatically check success.

## Files to Modify

- `src/cli/commands/close.rs` (exit code logic after processing loop)
- `src/error/mod.rs` (add NothingToDo variant with exit code 3)
- Tests in `tests/` (add test for exit code behavior)","acceptance_criteria":"1. `br close <already-closed-id>` returns exit code 3
2. `br close <nonexistent-id>` returns exit code 3
3. `br close <open-id>` returns exit code 0 (success)
4. `br close <open-id> <already-closed-id>` returns exit code 0 (partial success = success)
5. `br close <already-closed-1> <already-closed-2>` returns exit code 3 (all skipped)
6. JSON output includes `closed_count` field
7. Human-readable output still shows skip reasons
8. All existing tests pass","notes":"## Unit Tests (add to close command tests)

1. **test_close_already_closed_exit_code**: Create issue, close it, close again. Assert exit code 3.
2. **test_close_nonexistent_exit_code**: Close non-existent ID. Assert exit code 3.
3. **test_close_open_exit_code**: Create issue, close it. Assert exit code 0.
4. **test_close_mixed_exit_code**: Create 2 issues, close one, then close both. Assert exit code 0 (partial success).
5. **test_close_all_skipped_exit_code**: Create 2 issues, close both, then close both again. Assert exit code 3.
6. **test_close_json_closed_count**: Close an open issue with --json. Assert `closed_count: 1` in JSON output.

## E2E Test Script (scripts/test_close_exit_codes.sh)

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
PASS=0; FAIL=0

echo "=== br close exit code tests ==="

# Setup
TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d)
cd "$TMPDIR" && br init --force >/dev/null 2>&1
ID1=$(br q "Test issue 1")
ID2=$(br q "Test issue 2")

# Test 1: Close open issue -> exit 0
br close "$ID1" >/dev/null 2>&1; EC=$?
[[ $EC -eq 0 ]] && ((PASS++)) || { echo "FAIL: close open issue exit=$EC want=0"; ((FAIL++)); }

# Test 2: Close already-closed issue -> exit 3
br close "$ID1" >/dev/null 2>&1; EC=$?
[[ $EC -eq 3 ]] && ((PASS++)) || { echo "FAIL: close already-closed exit=$EC want=3"; ((FAIL++)); }

# Test 3: Close nonexistent -> exit 3
br close "fake-9999" >/dev/null 2>&1; EC=$?
[[ $EC -eq 3 ]] && ((PASS++)) || { echo "FAIL: close nonexistent exit=$EC want=3"; ((FAIL++)); }

# Test 4: Mixed (one open, one closed) -> exit 0
br close "$ID2" "$ID1" >/dev/null 2>&1; EC=$?
[[ $EC -eq 0 ]] && ((PASS++)) || { echo "FAIL: close mixed exit=$EC want=0"; ((FAIL++)); }

# Test 5: All already closed -> exit 3
br close "$ID1" "$ID2" >/dev/null 2>&1; EC=$?
[[ $EC -eq 3 ]] && ((PASS++)) || { echo "FAIL: close all-closed exit=$EC want=3"; ((FAIL++)); }

echo "Results: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed"
[[ $FAIL -eq 0 ]] && echo "ALL PASS" || exit 1

 {"id":"beads_rust-33yn","title":"Integrate rich output into close command","description":"## Command: br close <ID> [COMMENT]

### Traffic Level: HIGH
Closing issues - needs clear success confirmation and cascade information.

### Current Implementation
Location: src/cli/commands/close.rs
Output: 'Closed ID' confirmation

### Integration Steps
1. Show before/after status change
2. Display any cascading effects (unblocked issues)
3. Add comment preview if comment provided
4. Show resolution summary

### Success Display
```
✓ Closed beads_rust-abc1

  Status: open → closed
  Comment: Fixed in commit abc123
  
  Unblocked 2 issues:
  ├── beads_rust-def2  Now ready to work on
  └── beads_rust-ghi3  Now ready to work on
```

### Cascade Highlighting
When closing an issue unblocks others, this is IMPORTANT information:
- List newly-ready issues
- Highlight with green checkmarks
- Suggest next actions

### Warning Cases
If closing would leave orphaned children:
```
⚠ Warning: Closing beads_rust-abc1 will orphan 2 child issues:
  ├── beads_rust-def2  Still open
  └── beads_rust-ghi3  Still open

Proceed anyway? Use --force to skip this warning.
```

### Agent Mode (--robot)
Simple confirmation only.

---

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
Location: tests/cli/close_tests.rs

```rust
#[test]
fn test_close_shows_status_change() {
    let before = create_issue_with_status(Status::Open);
    let after = create_issue_with_status(Status::Closed);
    let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
    let output = render_close_result(&before, &after, &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("open"));
    assert!(output.contains("closed"));
    assert!(output.contains("→"));
}

#[test]
fn test_close_json_output_unchanged() {
    let current = run_close_json("test-id");
    let baseline = load_fixture("tests/fixtures/json_baseline/close.json");
    assert_json_schema_eq!(current, baseline);
}

#[test]
fn test_close_shows_unblocked_issues() {
    let unblocked = vec!["dep-1".to_string(), "dep-2".to_string()];
    let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
    let output = render_close_with_unblocked(&unblocked, &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("Unblocked"));
    assert!(output.contains("dep-1"));
    assert!(output.contains("dep-2"));
}

#[test]
fn test_close_with_comment() {
    let result = CloseResult { comment: Some("Fixed".to_string()), .. };
    let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
    let output = render_close_result(&result, &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("Fixed"));
}

#[test]
fn test_close_orphan_warning() {
    let orphans = vec!["child-1".to_string()];
    let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
    let output = render_close_warning_orphans(&orphans, &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("Warning") || output.contains("⚠"));
}

#[test]
fn test_close_robot_mode() {
    let result = CloseResult::default();
    let ctx = OutputContext::from_flags(true, false);
    let output = render_close_result(&result, &ctx);
    assert!(!contains_ansi_codes(&output));
}

#!/bin/bash

# Test 3: Shows unblocked issues

assert_contains "$CASCADE_OUTPUT" "Unblocked" "Should show unblocked"
assert_contains "$CASCADE_OUTPUT" "$BLOCKED" "Should show unblocked issue ID"

# Test 4: JSON output

JSON_OUTPUT=$(br close "$TEST_ID3" --json 2>&1)

# Test 5: Robot mode

ROBOT_OUTPUT=$(br close "$TEST_ID4" --robot 2>&1)
assert_no_ansi "$ROBOT_OUTPUT" "Robot mode should have no ANSI"

RICH_OUTPUT=$(script -q /dev/null br close "$TEST_ID5" 2>&1 || true)
log_pass "Rich mode renders"

log_success "=== Close command E2E: ALL PASSED ==="

 {"id":"beads_rust-biw","title":"search Command Implementation (FTS5)","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br search` command for full-text search across issues using SQLite FTS5 (Full-Text Search 5). This enables fast, relevance-ranked searches across issue titles, descriptions, and comments.

## CLI Interface
```
br search <query> [OPTIONS]

Arguments:
  <query>                   Search query (supports FTS5 syntax)

Options:
  -t, --type <TYPE>         Filter by issue type (bug, feature, task, epic, chore)
  -s, --status <STATUS>     Filter by status (open, closed, in_progress, etc.)
  -p, --priority <PRIORITY> Filter by priority (0-4)
  -l, --label <LABEL>       Filter by label (can be repeated)
  --limit <N>               Maximum results (default: 50)
  --offset <N>              Skip first N results
  --json                    Output as JSON
  --robot                   Machine-readable output
```

## Technical Requirements

### FTS5 Virtual Table
```sql
-- Create FTS5 table for search
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS issues_fts USING fts5(
    id,
    title,
    description,
    -- Use porter stemmer for English
    tokenize = 'porter unicode61 remove_diacritics 2'
);

-- Triggers to keep FTS in sync
CREATE TRIGGER issues_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON issues BEGIN
    INSERT INTO issues_fts(id, title, description)
    VALUES (NEW.id, NEW.title, NEW.description);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER issues_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON issues BEGIN
    DELETE FROM issues_fts WHERE id = OLD.id;
END;

CREATE TRIGGER issues_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON issues BEGIN
    DELETE FROM issues_fts WHERE id = OLD.id;
    INSERT INTO issues_fts(id, title, description)
    VALUES (NEW.id, NEW.title, NEW.description);
END;
```

### Search Implementation
```rust
impl SqliteStorage {
    pub fn search_issues(&self, query: &str, filter: &SearchFilter) -> Result<Vec<IssueWithScore>> {
        // FTS5 query with BM25 ranking
        let sql = r#"
            SELECT 
                i.*,
                bm25(issues_fts) as score
            FROM issues i
            JOIN issues_fts f ON i.id = f.id
            WHERE issues_fts MATCH ?1
              AND (?2 IS NULL OR i.status = ?2)
              AND (?3 IS NULL OR i.issue_type = ?3)
              AND (?4 IS NULL OR i.priority = ?4)
            ORDER BY score
            LIMIT ?5 OFFSET ?6
        "#;
        
        // ... execute query
    }
    
    pub fn rebuild_fts_index(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
        // For initial population or repair
        self.conn.execute("DELETE FROM issues_fts", [])?;
        self.conn.execute(
            "INSERT INTO issues_fts(id, title, description) 
             SELECT id, title, description FROM issues",
            []
        )?;
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### FTS5 Query Syntax Support
Users can use FTS5 extended query syntax:
- Simple terms: `authentication bug`
- Phrase search: `"login failed"`
- Prefix search: `auth*`
- Boolean operators: `auth AND NOT password`
- Column filters: `title:authentication`
- NEAR operator: `NEAR(login password, 5)`

### Comments Search (Optional)
```sql
-- Separate FTS table for comments
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS comments_fts USING fts5(
    id,
    issue_id,
    content,
    tokenize = 'porter unicode61'
);
```

## Output Formats

### Human-readable
```
Search results for "authentication bug":

1. [bd-abc12] [BUG] P0 Authentication fails silently
   ...the authentication system throws no error when...
   Score: 0.95

2. [bd-def34] [FEATURE] P2 Add OAuth authentication
   ...implement OAuth authentication for external...
   Score: 0.72

Found 2 matches (50ms)
```

### JSON
```json
{
  "query": "authentication bug",
  "results": [
    {
      "id": "bd-abc12",
      "title": "Authentication fails silently",
      "issue_type": "bug",
      "priority": 0,
      "score": 0.95,
      "snippet": "...the authentication system throws no error when..."
    }
  ],
  "total": 2,
  "elapsed_ms": 50
}
```

## Snippet Generation
Generate contextual snippets highlighting matching terms:
```rust
fn generate_snippet(text: &str, query: &str, max_len: usize) -> String {
    // Use FTS5 snippet() function or implement custom
    // Highlight with ANSI codes for terminal
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] FTS5 virtual table created during schema migration
- [ ] Triggers maintain FTS index on CRUD operations
- [ ] Search by title and description
- [ ] BM25 relevance ranking
- [ ] Filters combine with FTS (type, status, priority, labels)
- [ ] Pagination support (limit, offset)
- [ ] Snippet generation with term highlighting
- [ ] Human-readable and JSON output formats
- [ ] rebuild_fts_index() for repair/initial population
- [ ] Handle invalid FTS5 syntax gracefully with clear error message

## Unit Tests
- Test basic term search
- Test phrase search ("exact phrase")
- Test prefix search (term*)
- Test boolean operators (AND, OR, NOT)
- Test column filters (title:term)
- Test filter combinations with FTS
- Test BM25 ranking (more relevant results first)
- Test snippet generation
- Test empty results
- Test invalid FTS syntax error handling
- Test FTS index stays in sync after CRUD operations

## Dependencies
- Requires Database Schema & Migrations (FTS5 table creation)
- Requires SQLite Storage Layer Core
- Requires Model Types (IssueWithScore struct)

## Performance Considerations
- FTS5 queries are O(log n) not O(n)
- Index size approximately 2-3x text size
- Consider VACUUM after bulk imports
- Use LIMIT to avoid returning huge result sets

## Rationale
Full-text search is essential for discovering issues in large projects. FTS5 provides fast, relevance-ranked search with minimal code. This enables workflows like "find all issues mentioning authentication" which would otherwise require reading through hundreds of issues.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:17:10.810505346Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T07:50:01.787829331Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T07:50:01.787829331Z","close_reason":"Superseded by beads_rust-aww (LIKE-based search for classic bd parity)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}

-{"id":"beads_rust-bncj","title":"Fix JSONL import to preserve dependency and comment fields (GH#63)","description":"sync_dependencies_for_import hardcodes CURRENT_TIMESTAMP instead of JSONL values for created_at, created_by, metadata, thread_id. sync_comments_for_import lets SQLite auto-generate comment IDs instead of preserving from JSONL. Fix both for round-trip fidelity.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:18:27.885909534Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:18:27.885909534Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"beads_rust-bncj","title":"Fix JSONL import to preserve dependency and comment fields (GH#63)","description":"## Problem

`sync_dependencies_for_import` in `src/storage/sqlite.rs:3774` hardcodes `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` and `'import'` instead of using the Dependency struct's `created_at` and `created_by` fields. `sync_comments_for_import` at line 3798 lets SQLite auto-generate comment IDs instead of preserving the `id` from JSONL. This breaks round-trip fidelity when exporting and re-importing JSONL.

## Root Cause

The import functions were written as quick stubs that assumed only forward migration. The Dependency model (model/mod.rs ~line 500) has `created_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>` and `created_by: Option<String>` fields that are populated from JSONL but never passed to the INSERT statement. The Comment model has an `id: i32` field that is ignored during import.

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Fix sync_dependencies_for_import (sqlite.rs:3761-3780)

Change the INSERT statement from:
```sql
INSERT OR IGNORE INTO dependencies (issue_id, depends_on_id, type, created_at, created_by)
VALUES (?, ?, ?, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, 'import')
```
To:
```sql
INSERT OR IGNORE INTO dependencies (issue_id, depends_on_id, type, created_at, created_by, metadata, thread_id)
VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
```

Bind `dep.created_at.map(|dt| dt.to_rfc3339()).unwrap_or_else(|| Utc::now().to_rfc3339())` for created_at, `dep.created_by.as_deref().unwrap_or("import")` for created_by. Add metadata and thread_id bindings if those fields exist on the Dependency struct; otherwise add them to the model first.

### 2. Fix sync_comments_for_import (sqlite.rs:3787-3810)

Add comment ID preservation. The current INSERT:
```sql
INSERT INTO comments (issue_id, author, text, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
```
Should become:
```sql
INSERT OR REPLACE INTO comments (id, issue_id, author, text, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
```

Bind `comment.id` as the first parameter. Use `OR REPLACE` so that re-imports update existing comments rather than creating duplicates.

### 3. Verify round-trip in sync.rs (cli/commands/sync.rs:1189-1191)

The call site passes `&issue.dependencies` and `&issue.comments` -- confirm these structs are fully populated from JSONL deserialization. Check the JSONL parser in the same file to ensure dep/comment fields are read.

## Files to Modify

- `src/storage/sqlite.rs` lines 3761-3810 (both import functions)
- `src/model/mod.rs` ~line 500 (verify Dependency/Comment fields, add metadata/thread_id if missing)
- `tests/jsonl_import_export.rs` (add round-trip assertion test)","acceptance_criteria":"1. `br sync --import` preserves dependency created_at timestamps from JSONL (not CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)
2. `br sync --import` preserves dependency created_by values from JSONL (not hardcoded 'import')
3. `br sync --import` preserves comment IDs from JSONL (not auto-incremented)
4. Full export, import, export cycle produces identical JSONL (round-trip fidelity)
5. Backward compat: dependencies with missing created_at/created_by get sensible defaults
6. All existing tests pass unchanged
7. `cargo clippy` clean","notes":"## Unit Tests (add to tests/jsonl_import_export.rs)

1. **test_dependency_field_preservation**: Create issue with dependencies that have explicit created_at and created_by. Export to JSONL, import back. Assert created_at and created_by match original values exactly (not CURRENT_TIMESTAMP).

2. **test_comment_id_preservation**: Create issue with comments (known IDs). Export to JSONL, import back. Assert comment IDs match original values (not auto-incremented).

3. **test_dependency_defaults_when_missing**: Create dependency with None for created_at and created_by. Import. Assert created_at is close to now and created_by is "import" (backward compat).

4. **test_full_round_trip_fidelity**: Create complex issue with 3 deps (mixed Some/None fields) and 5 comments. Export, clear DB, import, export again. Diff the two JSONL outputs; they should be identical except for non-deterministic fields.

## E2E Test Script (scripts/test_jsonl_roundtrip.sh)

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'cleanup $TMPDIR' EXIT  # cleanup removes temp dir

echo "[1/5] Initialize workspace..."
cd "$TMPDIR" && br init --force 2>&1 | head -5

echo "[2/5] Create test issue with deps and comments..."
ID=$(br q "Round-trip test issue")
br comments add "$ID" --message "Test comment 1"
br comments add "$ID" --message "Test comment 2"

echo "[3/5] Export to JSONL..."
br sync --flush-only
cp .beads/issues.jsonl "$TMPDIR/export1.jsonl"
LINES1=$(wc -l < "$TMPDIR/export1.jsonl")
echo "  Exported $LINES1 lines"

echo "[4/5] Re-import from JSONL..."
br sync --import
br sync --flush-only
cp .beads/issues.jsonl "$TMPDIR/export2.jsonl"

echo "[5/5] Compare exports..."
if diff -u "$TMPDIR/export1.jsonl" "$TMPDIR/export2.jsonl" > "$TMPDIR/diff.txt" 2>&1; then
  echo "PASS: Round-trip produces identical JSONL"
else
  echo "FAIL: JSONL differs after round-trip"
  cat "$TMPDIR/diff.txt"
  exit 1
fi
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:18:27.885909534Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T07:42:19.159034781Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-btm","title":"Testing coverage: unit + E2E (no mocks, detailed logging)","description":"# Testing Coverage Epic

- All tests pass locally with cargo test and detailed logs available for failures.
- Each E2E scenario documents setup, steps, expected outputs, and log format.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T16:15:44.472404100Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T02:31:18.950748831Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T02:31:18.950748831Z","close_reason":"All children complete: E2E integration suite, unit test coverage expansion, and discovered bug fixed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
